ncs_standard_definition.description_short: A computational designer uses data and algorithms to create innovative designs and solutions.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Computational designers work at the intersection of art, design, and technology, using programming languages and data analysis to create interactive and immersive experiences.

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in design and technology can start by learning the basics of programming and design principles.

choice_maker.description: Students with a generic degree can bridge the gap by learning programming languages and design tools.

future_builder.description: Designers and developers already in the field can advance their skills by learning machine learning and data analysis.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2 years of experience in design or development

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can switch to computational design by learning programming languages and design principles.
portfolio_proof_required: Personal Projects in Design and Development

roadmap.title: Capstone & Portfolio
topics: Personal Project in Design and Development
